title: Policies (ToolGuard) Guard mode never completes and hits an internal timeout, and Generate mode fails the Agent build with an Anthropic thinking.thinking Field required error on claude-sonnet-5
author: abhigoel25
body:
### Bug Description This is a follow up to issue #13727, tested on a fresh install of Langflow 1.10.1 which already includes the fixes from that issue. Generation itself is now fixed. In Generate mode the Policies component runs the full pipeline and produces real guard code with passing tests, so the earlier pass #FIXME stub problem is resolved. However, two new problems make the component unusable in this Gmail flow. First, in Generate mode the Agent build fails with an Anthropic 400 error, messages.1.content.0.thinking.thinking Field required, on the claude-sonnet-5 model. The email is actually sent before the failure, so the tool call succeeds, but the flow still reports a build failure. Second, in Guard mode the flow never completes. It runs for roughly 750 to 900 seconds and then fails with Job queue not found for job_id, which is an internal timeout rather than a real result. Guard mode has been run more than 30 times and has never succeeded. ### Reproduction Reproduction 1. Build a flow with Chat Input, an Agent using claude-sonnet-5, a Gmail tool through Composio (GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL, OAuth2), the Policies component, and Chat Output. 2. Connect the Gmail Toolset output to the Policies Tools input, and the Policies Guarded Tools output to the Agent Tools input. 3. In Policies, set a Policies Project name, set the Language Model to claude-sonnet-5 with an API key, turn Enabled on, and enter a whitelist policy that allows sending email only to one approved address. 4. Set Activity to Generate and run the flow with the prompt below. 5. Observe that guards generate successfully and the email is actually sent, but the Agent build fails with the thinking error. 6. Set Activity to Guard and run the same prompt. 7. Observe that the run hangs for 750 to 900 seconds and then fails with Job queue not found for job_id. The prompt used was the following. Send email to abhinavgoel225@gmail.com with content "Hi" and subject line "Hi" The policy generated was named Restricted Recipient Whitelist, allowing only the approved address. What I Observed and Ruled Out Generation is fixed. On 1.10.1 the guard code is generated correctly with passing tests, so the stub problem from #13727 does not occur here. These are new issues. The Gmail tool and Composio auth work. The email is actually delivered during the Generate run, confirmed by receiving it, so the tool call and OAuth are fine. The thinking error is tied to the model. The error is an Anthropic extended thinking validation error on claude-sonnet-5. It appears in the follow up request to the model after the tool call, which points at how the Agent reconstructs the assistant message for an extended thinking model during a tool use turn. It may be avoidable with a non thinking model, which is worth testing. Guard mode hangs rather than failing fast. Unlike Generate, which fails quickly with the thinking error, Guard runs for many minutes before the internal timeout. This suggests a loop or a stuck runtime rather than a clean rejection. One possibility is that the Agent is repeatedly retrying after the same thinking error, but the internal cause could not be confirmed from the logs. ### Expected behavior Expected Behavior In Generate mode the Agent should complete without the model rejecting the request, and the flow should report success once the guards are generated and the email is sent. In Guard mode the component should load the generated guards, apply them to the Gmail tool, allow a send to the whitelisted address, and complete in a reasonable time. It should not hang or reach an internal timeout. Actual Behavior Generate mode The generation pipeline completes correctly. Step 1 produces the spec, and Step 2 generates the guard function and its tests, retries after some failed tests, and finishes with all tests passing. This is the expected behavior that was missing in #13727. The Agent build then fails with an Anthropic 400 error. ComponentBuildError: Error building Component Agent: Error code: 400 - {'type': 'error', 'error': {'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'message': 'messages.1.content.0.thinking.thinking: Field required'}, 'request_id': 'req_011Ccze2CdFtde46ieML2piF'}. This is an extended thinking formatting error. It indicates that when the Agent replays the prior assistant message back to claude-sonnet-5 after the tool call, it sends a thinking content block that is missing its required thinking field, which the Anthropic API rejects. Importantly, the email is sent before this error, so the tool call itself works. The failure is in the follow up request to the model. Guard mode The flow never completes. It runs for 750 to 900 seconds and then fails with an internal timeout. Flow build failed 900.8s Job not found: Job queue not found for job_id: c1b6e2c7-fd6a-43d6-b5e2-deb0b6405e59 The typical failure is at around 750 seconds with the same Job queue not found message. This has been reproduced more than 30 times, and Guard mode has never produced a result. Relevant Log Output Generate mode, showing the pipeline completing and the guard function generated with passing tests. [debug] Starting step 2 [debug] Starting... will save into tmp_toolguard\send_gmail_v11\Step_2 [debug] Generating tests iteration 'a' for tool gmail_send_email 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist'. [warning] 4 syntax errors in tests iteration 'a' in item 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist'. [debug] Generating tests iteration 'b' for tool gmail_send_email 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist'. 4 failed, 4 passed, 4 warnings in 0.75s [debug] 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist' guard function tests failed. Retrying... [debug] Improving guard function 'guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist'... (trial = 1.a) [info] Generated function guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist with 0 errors. 8 passed, 4 warnings in 0.31s Generate mode, the Agent build failure that follows. [debug] model = [{'name': 'claude-sonnet-5', 'provider': 'Anthropic', 'metadata': {'model_class': 'ChatAnthropic', 'context_length': 128000, ...}}] ComponentBuildError: Error building Component Agent: Error code: 400 - {'type': 'error', 'error': {'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'message': 'messages.1.content.0.thinking.thinking: Field required'}, 'request_id': 'req_011Ccze2CdFtde46ieML2piF'}. This report covers two symptoms that may share a root cause, since both involve the Agent running claude-sonnet-5 with the Policies component in the tool path. It would help to know whether the Guard mode hang is the Agent retrying after the same thinking.thinking rejection that fails Generate mode fast. A useful isolation step would be to run Guard mode with a non thinking model, or with extended thinking disabled, to see whether the hang disappears. If it does, the thinking message reconstruction in the Agent is the common cause. If Guard still hangs with a plain model, then the Guard runtime has a separate hang that is independent of the model. For reference, issue #13727 (closed) is the earlier problem where generation produced only stub guards. That is resolved in 1.10.1. This report is specifically about the remaining Generate mode thinking error and the Guard mode hang.
Full Generate mode log (trimmed to meaningful lines) [debug] Building Policies [debug] Starting step 1 [debug] model = [{'name': 'claude-sonnet-5', 'provider': 'Anthropic', 'metadata': {'model_class': 'ChatAnthropic', 'context_length': 128000}}] [debug] Step1 folder created [debug] create_spec(gmail_send_email) [debug] add_policy(gmail_send_email) [debug] review_policy(gmail_send_email) [debug] add_ref(gmail_send_email) [debug] reference_correctness(gmail_send_email) [debug] self_contained(gmail_send_email) [debug] review_policy_feasibility(gmail_send_email) [debug] example_creator(gmail_send_email) [debug] All tools done [debug] Step 1 Done [debug] Starting step 2 [debug] Step2 folder created [debug] Starting... will save into tmp_toolguard\send_gmail_v11\Step_2 [debug] Dependencies of 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist': [] [debug] Generating tests iteration 'a' for tool gmail_send_email 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist'. [warning] 4 syntax errors in tests iteration 'a' in item 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist'. [debug] Generating tests iteration 'b' for tool gmail_send_email 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist'. FAILED .../test_guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist.py::test_violation_recipient_email_not_whitelisted FAILED .../test_guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist.py::test_violation_cc_contains_non_whitelisted_address FAILED .../test_guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist.py::test_violation_bcc_contains_non_whitelisted_address FAILED .../test_guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist.py::test_violation_extra_recipients_contain_non_whitelisted_addresses 4 failed, 4 passed, 4 warnings in 0.75s [debug] 'Restricted Recipient Whitelist' guard function tests failed. Retrying... [debug] Improving guard function 'guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist'... (trial = 1.a) [info] Generated function guard_restricted_recipient_whitelist with 0 errors. 8 passed, 4 warnings in 0.31s ComponentBuildError: Error building Component Agent: Error code: 400 - {'type': 'error', 'error': {'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'message': 'messages.1.content.0.thinking.thinking: Field required'}, 'request_id': 'req_011Ccze2CdFtde46ieML2piF'}.
### Who can help? @italojohnny @ogabrielluiz @nicoloboschi ### Operating System OS Windows ### Langflow Version 1.10.1 ### Python Version 3.12 ### Screenshot [Policies (Email).json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29983552/Policies.Email.json) ### Flow File _No response_

title: docs: telemetry page's event list omits component_inputs (which sends configured input values)
author: kobihikri
body:
### What I noticed

The telemetry docs (`docs/docs/Develop/contributing-telemetry.mdx`) present what reads as a complete list of event types (Run, Shutdown, Version, Email, Playground, Component, Exception, Deployment provider, Deployment) and say:

> *"Langflow telemetry doesn't collect any personal information or sensitive data. All telemetry data is anonymized and used solely for improving Langflow."*

Looking at `services/telemetry/schema.py`, there are two payload types that don't appear in that list:

- **`ComponentInputsPayload`** (line 67) — its docstring says *"Separate payload for component input values, joined via component_run_id"* and it carries `component_inputs: dict[str, Any]` (line 92), i.e. the configured input **values**, not just the component name/duration that the documented `Component` event covers.
- A separate **component-index** payload alongside it.

### Why I'm raising it

I don't want to overstate this — it looks like there's some gating on which input types are tracked, which you'd know far better than I do, and I couldn't fully trace that myself. But since the docs present a complete list and promise "no sensitive data," it seems worth having `component_inputs` (and the index event) documented too, so a reader can see that configured input values are among what's sent. Would you consider adding them to the telemetry page? Happy to help draft it once I understand the gating, if that's useful.

<sub>AI-assisted; the schema.py references I verified myself against `main`.</sub>

title: Langflow MCP tools advertise multiple input fields but only pass `input_value` during execution
author: kmnis
body:
### Bug Description

When a flow is exposed as a tool through Langflow's built-in MCP server, tool discovery generates an `inputSchema` containing all visible, non-advanced fields from the flow's input nodes.

But, when the MCP tool is called, only the value supplied for `input_value` reaches the flow. Other arguments advertised in the schema are silently ignored.

In my case, the generated schema included:

- `input_value`
- `backend_token`
- `backend_url`

When I called the MCP tool with values for all three parameters, only `input_value` was populated. Logging with `self.log()` inside the component showed empty values for `backend_token` and `backend_url`.

The mismatch seems to be between these two functions:

### Schema generation

[src/backend/base/langflow/helpers/flow.py:json_schema_from_flow()](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/def832f409c01f0acd3937b9317dde03d0273552/src/backend/base/langflow/helpers/flow.py#L540-L548)

The function identifies every vertex marked as an input:

```python
input_nodes = [vertex for vertex in graph.vertices if vertex.is_input]
````

It then iterates through the input-node template and adds every visible, non-advanced field to the generated MCP JSON Schema. Therefore, fields such as `backend_token` and `backend_url` are advertised as supported MCP tool arguments.

### Tool execution

[src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/mcp_utils.py:handle_call_tool()](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/v1.10.2/src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/mcp_utils.py#L256-L301)

The relevant execution logic copies the submitted arguments but constructs `SimplifiedAPIRequest` using only `input_value` and `session_id`:

```python
processed_inputs = dict(arguments)

input_request = SimplifiedAPIRequest(
    input_value=processed_inputs.get("input_value", ""),
    session_id=session_id,
)
```

The remaining values in `processed_inputs` are not mapped to their originating input-node fields, passed as tweaks, or otherwise forwarded to `simple_run_flow()`.

This creates a mismatch between the published MCP tool contract and actual execution behavior:

1. `json_schema_from_flow()` advertises multiple named input fields.
2. The MCP client submits values for those advertised fields.
3. `handle_call_tool()` forwards only `input_value` and `session_id`.
4. Every other advertised argument is silently discarded.

I tried this on Langflow 1.9.2 but the latest version v1.10.2 also has the same issue.

### Reproduction

Code is proprietary but here are some steps to reproduce:
 
1. Create a flow containing an input component and a Chat Output component.
2. Configure the input component with three visible string fields:

   - `input_value`
   - `backend_token`
   - `backend_url`

3. Add logging inside the input component or a downstream custom component to inspect the received values:

```python
self.log(f"input_value: {self.input_value!r}")
self.log(f"backend_token: {self.backend_token!r}")
self.log(f"backend_url: {self.backend_url!r}")
````

4. Import the flow into Langflow through the dashboard.

5. Expose the folder/project containing the flow through Langflow's built-in MCP server.

6. Run MCP tool discovery using an MCP client, MCP Inspector, or a `tools/list` request.

The discovered tool schema includes all three fields, similar to:

```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "input_value": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "backend_token": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "backend_url": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "session_id": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  }
}
```

7. Invoke the discovered tool with values for all three fields. For example, after initializing the MCP session:

```bash
curl -X POST "$MCP_URL" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $MCP_SESSION_ID" \
  -H "x-api-key: $LANGFLOW_API_KEY" \
  -d '{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": 2,
    "method": "tools/call",
    "params": {
      "name": "YOUR_FLOW_TOOL_NAME",
      "arguments": {
        "input_value": "test request",
        "backend_token": "example-token",
        "backend_url": "https://backend.example.com"
      }
    }
  }'
```

8. Inspect the Langflow logs.

Observed values:

```text
input_value: 'test request'
backend_token: ''
backend_url: ''
```

Only `input_value` is populated. The values supplied for `backend_token` and `backend_url` do not reach the corresponding component fields.

### Expected behavior

Every argument advertised in the MCP tool's `inputSchema` should be passed to its corresponding input-node field when the tool is called.

For example, a tool invocation containing:

```json
{
  "input_value": "test request",
  "backend_token": "example-token",
  "backend_url": "https://backend.example.com"
}
````

should result in the flow receiving all three values:

```text
input_value = "test request"
backend_token = "example-token"
backend_url = "https://backend.example.com"
```

If Langflow's MCP execution path is intentionally limited to `input_value` and `session_id`, the generated `inputSchema` should advertise only those supported parameters.

Langflow should not advertise additional parameters in the MCP schema and then silently discard them during execution.

Ideally, schema generation should preserve a mapping between each MCP argument and its originating node ID and field name. The execution handler could then use that mapping to populate the appropriate flow inputs while avoiding collisions when multiple input nodes contain fields with the same name.

### Who can help?

@italojohnny 

### Operating System

RHEL 8.10

### Langflow Version

1.9.2

### Python Version

3.11

### Screenshot

_No response_

### Flow File

_No response_

title: Feature Request: Integrate ContextForge for Enterprise MCP Isolation and Modular Efficiency
author: YamonBot
body:
### Feature Request

I propose integrating **ContextForge** into Langflow to optimize our enterprise-level architecture.

While Langflow currently offers robust MCP (Model Context Protocol) isolation and modular system capabilities, we often find ourselves re-implementing context management logic that already exists in specialized solutions. Integrating ContextForge would allow us to:

* Streamline context orchestration across complex workflows.
* Minimize "reinventing the wheel" for sophisticated state management.
* Focus development efforts on core agentic features rather than maintaining redundant low-level infrastructure.


### Motivation

This request stems from the desire to reduce technical debt and accelerate the development cycle. Currently, when building complex enterprise agents, we encounter inefficiencies in managing cross-component context consistency.

By leveraging ContextForge, we can offload these standard, high-complexity tasks to a dedicated solution, allowing the Langflow team to prioritize unique value propositions. This approach aligns with our goal of building a more maintainable, scalable, and modular AI infrastructure.

title: CVSS fix for CVE-2026-55255
author: todb
body: Hi! In https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/security/advisories/GHSA-qrpv-q767-xqq2 you list `AC:H` (Access Complexity: High). This seems wrongly generous. The attacker needs to first learn the targeted flow_id, which, on its own, is an impossible-to-guess UUID. This strikes me as `AC:L`. Yeah?

title: StrInput None
author: jbcl-bro
body:
# Problematic

StrInput values resolving to None at runtime in Custom Component (LangFlow 1.10.0)


I am encountering an issue with a custom component in LangFlow version 1.10.0 (running via the official Docker image on Python 3.14).

The Problem:
Values entered by the user in the UI via a StrInput field are not being captured or passed correctly when the Flow executes. At runtime, the input argument consistently resolves to None. Field concerned : "tube_config_name" !

As a fallback mechanism in my code, since the value is evaluated as None or empty, the component generates a str(uuid.uuid4()) instead of using the user-provided string.

# Environment Details

---

## 1. LangFlow Version

* **Version:** 1.10.0 (based on the Docker image `langflowai/langflow:1.10.0`)

---

## 2. Environment and Versioning

* **Operating System (Container):** Linux
* **Python Version (Container):** 3.14 (specified by the Dockerfile)
* **Project Python Requirement:** `>=3.13` (per `pyproject.toml`)
* **Additional Python Libraries (installed in the container):** `pymysql`, `faker`
* **Project-Specific Python Dependencies (per `pyproject.toml`):** `datamodel-code-generator>=0.59.0`, `pyyaml>=6.0.3`, `streamlit>=1.56.0`

---

## 3. Database Configuration

* **Type:** SQLite
* **Location (inside the container):** `/data/langflow.db`
* **Persistence (host mapping):** `./langflow_data` on the host is mounted to `/data` inside the container.

---

## 4. Docker Configuration

* **Docker Compose Version:** 3.8
* **Service Name:** `langflow`
* **Container Name:** `langflow_server`
* **Network Mode:** `host` (the container shares the host's network stack)
* **Ports:** Not explicitly mapped, but `host` mode directly uses the host's ports (e.g., LangFlow's default port 7860).
* **Restart Policy:** `unless-stopped`
* **Healthcheck:** `http://localhost:7860/health`

---

## 5. Custom Components and Integrations

* **Custom Components Path (inside the container):** `/app/langflow/components`
* **Host Mapping for Custom Components:** `./my_custom_components` on the host is mounted to `/app/langflow/components` inside the container.
* **Python Path for Custom Components:** `PYTHONPATH` includes `/app/langflow/components/QQ1-AG`.

---

## 6. Security and Logging

* `DO_NOT_TRACK=true`
* `LANGFLOW_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false`
* `LANGFLOW_LOG_LEVEL=INFO`
* `LANGFLOW_LOG_FILE=/app/langflow/components/langflow.log`
* `LANGFLOW_SECRET_KEY` is defined.

## 7. Component

```python
from langflow.io import SecretStrInput, StrInput, Output, DropdownInput, HandleInput, BoolInput, MultilineInput
from langflow.schema import Data
from langflow.schema.message import Message
from langflow.custom import Component
from typing import Any
# portée de classe

import requests
import json
import logging
import os
import random
import sys
import time
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
import urllib.parse
import json
import importlib.util
    
    
class TubeRegistryComponent(Component):
    display_name = "Tube Registry"
    name         = "TubeRegistryComponent"
    description  = (
        "Enregistre un serveur de tubes et charge la liste des tubes disponibles globalement "
        "sous la variable globale TUBE_LIST_<NOM DE LA CONFIGURATION>."
    )
    icon = "database"

    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        _params = kwargs.get('_parameters', {})
        logging.warning(
            f"[TubeRegistry][INIT] kwargs keys={list(kwargs.keys())} "
            f"| _parameters={_params}"
        )
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        logging.warning(
            f"[TubeRegistry][POST-INIT] self._attributes="
            f"{getattr(self, '_attributes', '__ABSENT__')!r}"
        )

    inputs = [
        MultilineInput(
            name="tube_config_name",
            display_name="Nom de la configuration",
            info="Nom unique pour identifier cette configuration. Si non renseigné, un UUID aléatoire sera généré.",
            required=False,
            value="",
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="url",
            display_name="URL API",
            info="URL du serveur de tubes (ex: http://127.0.0.1 ou localhost).",
            required=True,
            value="http://127.0.0.1",
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="token",
            display_name="Token API (X-API-Key)",
            info="Clé d'API / Token pour s'authentifier auprès du serveur de tubes.",
            required=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="Resident",
            display_name="Tube Résident ?",
            info="",
            required=True,
            value=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="spool_path",
            display_name="Chemin du Spool",
            info="Chemin vers le dossier spool pour le dépôt de commandes (ex: /tools/browser-bot/commands).",
            required=True,
            value="/tools/browser-bot/commands",
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="port",
            display_name="Port",
            info="Port du serveur API (ex: 6666).",
            required=False,
            value="6666",
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="profile",
            display_name="Profil Chrome",
            info="Profil Chrome à utiliser pour l'exécution.",
            options=["default"],
            value="default",
            required=False,
            refresh_button=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="signal",
            display_name="Signal / Déclencheur",
            info="Permet de déclencher l'affichage du State après l'exécution d'un autre composant.",
            input_types=["JSON", "Message", "LanguageModel", "BaseChatModel"],
            advanced=True,
            required=False,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="extra_params",
            display_name="Paramètres Avancés (Multiples)",
            info="Connectez ici un ou plusieurs composants (Data, Dict, Message) pour surcharger le payload (ex: paramètres NotebookLM).",
            input_types=["Dict", "Data", "Message", "JSON", "Text", "Str"],
            is_list=True,
            advanced=True,
            required=False,
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Configuration (Data)", name="config_data", method="build_config_data"),
    ]
    
    def unserializerHandleInput(self, attr) -> str:
        try:
            val = getattr(self, attr, "")
        except AttributeError:
            val = ""
            
        if val is None:
            return ""
        if hasattr(val, "text"):
            return val.text or ""
        if hasattr(val, "data") and isinstance(val.data, dict) and "text" in val.data:
            return val.data["text"] or ""
            
        res = str(val).strip()
        if res == "None":
            return ""
        return res

    def _get_shared(self):
        """Charge lib/shared.py depuis son chemin absolu dans le conteneur Docker.
        NOTE: __file__ est inutilisable ici car LangFlow exécute le composant
        comme une chaîne de caractères (<string>), donc on utilise le chemin
        absolu connu dans le conteneur (volume monté dans docker-compose.yml).
        """

        shared_path = "/app/langflow/components/REFUND/lib/shared.py"
        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("shared", shared_path)
        mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
        spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
        return mod

    def _resolve_config(self) -> dict:
        raw_name = self.unserializerHandleInput("tube_config_name")
        if not raw_name:
            raw_name = self.unserializerHandleInput("config_name")
        
        raw_name = str(raw_name).strip()

        logging.warning(f"[TubeRegistry][DIAG-v4] self.config_name={raw_name!r}")

        if raw_name:
            resolved_config_name = raw_name
        else:
            if not hasattr(self, "_fallback_config_name"):
                self._fallback_config_name = str(uuid.uuid4())
            resolved_config_name = self._fallback_config_name

        url = self.url.strip() if getattr(self, "url", None) else ""
        port = self.port.strip() if getattr(self, "port", None) else ""
        token = self.token if getattr(self, "token", None) else ""
        spool_path = self.spool_path.strip() if getattr(self, "spool_path", None) else ""
        profile = self.profile if getattr(self, "profile", None) else "default"
        if url and not url.startswith("http://") and not url.startswith("https://"):
            url = "http://" + url

        if url:
            parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
            host = parsed.hostname or parsed.path
            if not host and parsed.netloc:
                host = parsed.netloc.split(":")[0]

            if port:
                netloc = f"{host}:{port}"
            else:
                netloc = parsed.netloc or host

            scheme = parsed.scheme or "http"
            api_base_url = f"{scheme}://{netloc}"
        else:
            api_base_url = ""

        config = {
            "config_name": resolved_config_name,
            "url": api_base_url,
            "token": token,
            "spool_path": spool_path,
            "port": port,
            "profile": profile,
        }
        try:
            shared = self._get_shared()
            tubes_data = shared.get_tubes_list(api_base_url, token) if api_base_url and token else {}
            tubes = tubes_data.get("tubes", [])
            
            ctx = shared.get_context(self)
            if ctx is not None:
                if "REGISTRY" not in ctx or not isinstance(ctx["REGISTRY"], dict):
                    ctx["REGISTRY"] = {}
                ctx["REGISTRY"][resolved_config_name] = {
                    "config": config,
                    "instance": self,
                    "tubes": tubes,
                    "registry_type": "TUBE"
                }
                logging.info(f"[TubeRegistry] Config '{resolved_config_name}' enregistrée (depuis _resolve_config).")
        except Exception as e:
            logging.debug(f"[TubeRegistry] Erreur d'enregistrement contextuel : {e}")

        return config
    def resolve_model(self, model_input: str, prompt_text: str = "", tube: str = None) -> tuple:
        """
        Résout le nom de modèle et le type de sortie attendu.

        Si un nom de tube est fourni, le media_type est lu depuis le State de LangFlow :
            - image  → expected_output = 'image'
            - video  → expected_output = 'video'
            - audio  → expected_output = 'audio'
            - autre  → expected_output = 'text'

        Si aucun tube n'est précisé, retourne (model_input, 'text') par défaut.
        Retourne un tuple (actual_model, expected_output).
        """
        expected_output = "text"
        actual_model = model_input or "auto"

        if tube:
            config = self._resolve_config()
            config_name = config["config_name"]
            
            shared = self._get_shared()
            registry_configs = shared.get_context_element(self, "REGISTRY", {})
            my_registry = registry_configs.get(config_name, {})
            tubes_list = my_registry.get("tubes", [])
            
            if not tubes_list:
                tubes_list = []

            if isinstance(tubes_list, list):
                for t in tubes_list:
                    if t.get("name") == tube:
                        media_type = t.get("media_type", "OTHER").strip().lower()
                        if media_type == "image":
                            expected_output = "image"
                        elif media_type == "video":
                            expected_output = "video"
                        elif media_type == "audio":
                            expected_output = "audio"
                        else:
                            expected_output = "text"
                        logging.debug(
                            f"[TubeRegistry] Tube '{tube}' → media_type='{media_type}' → expected_output='{expected_output}'"
                        )
                        break
                else:
                    logging.debug(
                        f"[TubeRegistry] Tube '{tube}' introuvable dans la liste des tubes. "
                        f"expected_output laissé à 'text'."
                    )

        return actual_model, expected_output
    def transmit_prompt(self, **kwargs) -> dict:
        config = self._resolve_config()
        is_resident = getattr(self, "Resident", False)
        have_custom_params = getattr(self, "extra_params", False)
        if have_custom_params :
            payload = self.extra_params.dict()
        else :
            prompt_text_str = str(kwargs.pop("prompt_text", "")).strip()
            timeout = kwargs.pop("timeout", 240)
            commands_dir_str = kwargs.pop("commands_dir_str", None)
            tube = kwargs.pop("tube", None)
            model = kwargs.pop("model", "auto")
            upload_file = kwargs.pop("upload_file", None)
            action = kwargs.pop("action", None) # Retiré s'il existe
            account = kwargs.pop("account", None)
            actual_model, expected_output = self.resolve_model(model, prompt_text_str, tube=tube)
            payload = {
                "api_base_url": config.get("url", ""),
                "token": config.get("token", ""),
                "account": config.get("profile", account),
                "actual_model": actual_model,
                "expected_output": expected_output,
            }
            optional_data = {
                "prompt_text": prompt_text_str,
                "timeout": timeout,
                "commands_dir_str": commands_dir_str,
                "tube": tube,
                "upload_file": upload_file,
            }
            payload.update({k: v for k, v in optional_data.items() if v is not None and v != ""})
            payload.update(kwargs)
        if is_resident:
            if commands_dir_str is None:
                commands_dir_str = config.get("spool_path", "/tools/browser-bot/commands")

            commands_dir = Path(commands_dir_str)
            commands_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

            req_id = f"{int(time.time())}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
            cmd_in_path  = commands_dir / f"req_{req_id}.json"
            cmd_out_path = commands_dir / f"resp_{req_id}.json"

            extra_raw = getattr(self, "extra_params", {})
            if not extra_raw :
                payload = {
                    "action":          action,
                    "expected_output": expected_output,
                    "upload_file":     upload_file or [],
                    "timeout":         timeout,
                }
                
                if model != "auto":
                    payload["model"] = actual_model
                    
                if prompt_text_str:
                    payload["text"] = prompt_text_str
                payload["req_id"] = req_id

            else: 
                payload["req_id"] = req_id

            time.sleep(random.uniform(1, 10))
            try:
                with cmd_in_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                    json.dump(payload, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
            except Exception as e:
                return {"status": "error", "error": f"Erreur d'écriture de la commande : {e}"}

            start_time = time.time()
            while True:
                if time.time() - start_time > (timeout + 20):
                    if cmd_in_path.exists():
                        try: cmd_in_path.unlink()
                        except Exception: pass
                    return {"status": "error", "error": f"Timeout ({timeout}s) dépassé pour req {req_id}."}
                    
                if cmd_out_path.exists():
                    time.sleep(0.5)
                    try:
                        with cmd_out_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                            response_data = json.load(f)
                        time.sleep(random.uniform(1, 10))
                        return response_data
                    except Exception as e:
                        return {"status": "error", "error": f"Erreur lecture résultat : {e}"}
                time.sleep(random.uniform(1, 10))
        else:
            extra_raw = getattr(self, "extra_params", [])
            if not extra_raw :
                payload = {
                    "tube": tube or "",
                    "prompt": [prompt_text_str] if prompt_text_str else [],
                    "account": account,
                    "desktop": True,
                    "headless": kwargs.pop("headless", False),
                }
                
                if "download_dir" in kwargs:
                    payload["download_dir"] = kwargs.pop("download_dir")
                elif "download_dir" in config:
                    payload["download_dir"] = config["download_dir"]
                payload.update(kwargs)
                
            else: 
                payload = extra_raw

            headers = {
                "X-API-Key": self.token,
                "Content-Type": "application/json"
            }
            try:
                resp = requests.post(f"{self.api_base_url}/run", json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=10)
                resp.raise_for_status()
                job_data = resp.json()
                job_id = job_data.get("job_id")
                if not job_id:
                    return {"status": "error", "error": f"Pas de job_id retourné par l'API: {job_data}"}
            except Exception as e:
                return {"status": "error", "error": f"Erreur POST API /run : {e}"}
            start_time = time.time()
            while True:
                if time.time() - start_time > (timeout + 20):
                    return {"status": "error", "error": f"Timeout API ({timeout}s) dépassé pour le job {job_id}."}
                    
                try:
                    status_resp = requests.get(f"{self.api_base_url}/status/{job_id}", headers=headers, timeout=5)
                    if status_resp.status_code == 200:
                        st_data = status_resp.json()
                        st = st_data.get("status")
                        if st in ["completed", "failed", "error"]:
                            return st_data.get("result", st_data)
                except Exception:
                    pass # Ignore et retente
                    
                time.sleep(2)

    def build_config_data(self) -> Data:
        logging.warning(
            f"[TubeRegistry][DIAG-v4] self.tube_config_name={getattr(self, 'tube_config_name', '__ABSENT__')!r} "
            f"| inputs[0].value={next((i.value for i in self.inputs if i.name == 'tube_config_name'), '__ABSENT__')!r}"
        )
        config = self._resolve_config()
        config_name = config["config_name"]
        api_base_url = config["url"]
        token = config["token"]
        spool_path = config["spool_path"]
        port = config["port"]
        profile = config["profile"]

        shared = self._get_shared()
        tubes_data = shared.get_tubes_list(api_base_url, token)
        tubes = tubes_data.get("tubes", [])

        return Data(
            data={
                "config_name": config_name,
                "url": api_base_url,
                "token": token,
                "spool_path": spool_path,
                "port": port,
                "profile": profile,
                "tubes": tubes,
            }
        )

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        """
        Met à jour dynamiquement la liste des profils Chrome (dropdown)
        en interrogeant l'API du serveur de tubes.
        """

        def get_value(field_data):
            if isinstance(field_data, dict):
                return field_data.get("value", "")
            return str(field_data or "")

        url = get_value(build_config.get("url"))
        token = get_value(build_config.get("token"))
        port = get_value(build_config.get("port"))

        if field_name == "url":
            url = str(field_value or "")
        elif field_name == "token":
            token = str(field_value or "")
        elif field_name == "port":
            port = str(field_value or "")

        url_str = url.strip()
        port_str = port.strip()
        if url_str:
            if not url_str.startswith("http://") and not url_str.startswith("https://"):
                url_str = "http://" + url_str

            parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(url_str)
            host = parsed.hostname or parsed.path
            if not host and parsed.netloc:
                host = parsed.netloc.split(":")[0]

            if port_str:
                netloc = f"{host}:{port_str}"
            else:
                netloc = parsed.netloc or host

            scheme = parsed.scheme or "http"
            api_base_url = f"{scheme}://{netloc}"
        else:
            api_base_url = ""

        profiles_list = []
        if api_base_url and token:
            try:
                shared = self._get_shared()
                res = shared.get_profiles_list(api_base_url, token)
                profiles_list = res.get("profiles", [])
            except Exception as e:
                logging.error(f"Error fetching profiles: {e}")

        if "profile" not in build_config:
            build_config["profile"] = {}

        if profiles_list:
            build_config["profile"]["options"] = sorted(profiles_list)
        else:
            build_config["profile"]["options"] = ["default"]

        return build_config
```

title: MCP server config: lost updates and list divergence with more than one backend worker/replica
author: thesaadmirza
body:
## Bug Description

Each user's MCP servers are stored as a per-user JSON file (`_mcp_servers_<user_id>.json`) written through the files API, and edits are serialized only by an **in-process** lock:

- `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v2/mcp.py:30` — `_update_server_locks: dict[str, asyncio.Lock] = defaultdict(asyncio.Lock)`
- `update_server()` (`mcp.py:323`) performs a non-atomic **read-modify-write of the whole file** under `async with _update_server_locks[str(current_user.id)]` (`mcp.py:335`). The write itself is delete-then-recreate in `upload_user_file`, so it is not atomic either.

Because that lock lives in one process's memory, it does **not** coordinate across Uvicorn workers or replicas. With more than one backend process, two problems appear:

1. **Lost updates.** Two concurrent edits to the same user's list both read the old file, modify independently, and the last write wins — silently dropping servers.
2. **List divergence.** With the default local-disk storage, each replica keeps its own copy of the file, so `GET /api/v2/mcp/servers` returns different lists depending on which replica answers.

Net effect: MCP server management is unsafe above a single worker/replica, so Langflow cannot be run HA for MCP. (Note this bites at `LANGFLOW_WORKERS>1` too, not only multi-pod.)

## Reproduction

```
1. Run Langflow with >1 process sharing one database
   (either LANGFLOW_WORKERS=2, or 2+ replicas behind a load balancer).
2. For a single user, fire N concurrent
   POST /api/v2/mcp/servers/{name}  with N distinct names (e.g. N=30).
3. GET /api/v2/mcp/servers
```

Observed: far fewer than N servers persist — the concurrent read-modify-write on the shared file loses updates, because the lock at `mcp.py:30` does not span processes. With local-disk storage the list also flickers between replicas (divergence). Expected: all N distinct servers persist and the list is identical on every replica.

## Expected behavior

Concurrent additions of distinct MCP servers for a user all persist, and the server list is identical across replicas — MCP config is safe at any worker/replica count.

## Which version are you on

`release-1.11.0` (1.11.0) and earlier; rooted in the file + in-process-lock design, not a regression.

## Additional context — proposed fix (seeking direction before opening a PR)

The file approach has been hardened before (#8847, #9014, #12762); this is the remaining cross-process gap. I'd like to close it by moving MCP server config into a small database table:

- A `mcp_server` table, one row per server, unique on `(user_id, name)`; `get_server_list` / `get_server` / `update_server` become per-row reads/upserts and the in-process lock is removed. This eliminates both the lost-update race and the list divergence at any worker/replica count, removes the shared-storage requirement, and lets secret values (`env` / `headers`) be encrypted at rest instead of stored in plaintext on disk.
- Backwards compatible: the REST shape (`{"mcpServers": {...}}`) and the function signatures are unchanged, and a startup backfill imports existing `_mcp_servers_*.json` files (idempotent; the file is kept for rollback).

I have a working implementation with tests (unit + a real 4-process shared-DB run where 40/40 concurrent adds survive; alembic migration validated on SQLite and Postgres 16, Expand phase) and would like to open a PR against `release-1.11.0`. Would you accept this approach?

title: Langflow Composio component fails due to incompatible composio and composio-client SDK versions
author: CHuiV123
body: ### Bug Description When using the Langflow Composio component (e.g., Gmail tool), component initialization fails with the following error: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'composio_client.types.tool_router_create_session_params' The error occurs during import of: lfx.base.composio.composio_base.ComposioBaseComponent which internally imports: from composio import Composio and fails inside: composio/core/models/mcp.py There is a version incompatibility between installed SDK packages: composio==0.9.2 composio-client==1.41.0 The installed composio-client no longer contains: composio_client.types.tool_router_create_session_params but the composio package still attempts to import it. This indicates: SDK API structure mismatch Breaking change in composio-client not reflected in composio version dependency constraints ### Reproduction Install the lastest Langflow, then configure Composio Tools, or Gmail or any composio component under bundles. Current fix: (Tested and worked from my side) pip uninstall composio composio-client -y pip install composio --upgrade pip install composio-client --upgrade Expected fix from Langflow side: Langflow should explicitly pin and maintain compatibility between composio and composio-client SDK versions, or provide a validated supported version pair. The current integration breaks due to uncoordinated version changes in Composio’s SDK. ### Expected behavior Component can be successfully configure to use. ### Who can help? @ogabrielluiz @italojohnny ### Operating System Windows 11 ### Langflow Version 1.10.1 ### Python Version 3.12 ### Screenshot ### Flow File _No response_

title: [BUG] SequentialTaskAgentComponent skips convert_llm at Agent factory; sibling factory applies it
author: Fr3ya
body:
### Bug Description

`SequentialTaskAgentComponent.build_agent_and_task` at [`src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_task_agent.py:107-127`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_task_agent.py#L107-L127) constructs `crewai.Agent(llm=self.llm, ...)` with the raw `LanguageModel` handle from its `HandleInput`. It never calls `convert_llm()` (the helper that wraps a LangChain `BaseChatModel` into a `crewai.LLM`). The sibling factory `CrewAIAgentComponent.build_output` at [`src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/crewai.py:83-108`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/crewai.py#L83-L108) (line 93) does call `convert_llm(self.llm)` at the same construction point, so the two Agent factories in the same package disagree on whether the Agent leaves the factory with a CrewAI-typed `.llm` or a raw LangChain handle.

the conversion is performed lazily by [`BaseCrewComponent.get_tasks_and_agents`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/base/agents/crewai/crew.py#L158-L169) (line 166: `agent.llm = convert_llm(agent.llm)`) only when the Agent is consumed via a `SequentialCrewComponent`. Any other consumer of the `SequentialTask.agent` port — i.e. any visual-graph edge from this factory's output that doesn't terminate at a `SequentialCrew` — receives an Agent whose `.llm` is the wrong type for CrewAI's executor. 

**Important caveat.** `SequentialTaskAgentComponent` declares `legacy = True` ([line 11](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_task_agent.py#L11)), so the framework already discourages new flows from using it. If the plan is to delete it, this report is moot; if it stays in the codebase to support saved flows, the asymmetry with the non-legacy sibling is worth resolving with the one-line fix.


### Reproduction

The reproducer below AST-parses the two factories and asserts the asymmetry plus the lazy-wrap deferral downstream. No `langflow` / `crewai` install is required.

```python
from __future__ import annotations
import ast, sys
from pathlib import Path

REPO = Path("/path/to/cloned/langflow")    # adjust to your checkout
FACTORY = REPO/"src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_task_agent.py"
SIBLING = REPO/"src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/crewai.py"
WRAPPER = REPO/"src/lfx/src/lfx/base/agents/crewai/crew.py"

def find_method(tree, cls, name):
    for n in ast.walk(tree):
        if isinstance(n, ast.ClassDef) and n.name == cls:
            for s in n.body:
                if isinstance(s, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)) and s.name == name:
                    return s
def find_agent_call(fn):
    for s in ast.walk(fn):
        if isinstance(s, ast.Call) and isinstance(s.func, ast.Name) and s.func.id == "Agent":
            return s
def kwarg_src(call, name):
    for kw in call.keywords:
        if kw.arg == name: return ast.unparse(kw.value)

# Check 1: factory passes self.llm raw (no convert_llm)
fn = find_method(ast.parse(FACTORY.read_text()), "SequentialTaskAgentComponent", "build_agent_and_task")
factory_llm = kwarg_src(find_agent_call(fn), "llm")
print(f"factory llm kwarg          = {factory_llm!r}")
assert factory_llm == "self.llm", factory_llm
assert "convert_llm" not in FACTORY.read_text(), "factory unexpectedly mentions convert_llm"

# Check 2: sibling factory DOES wrap with convert_llm
fn = find_method(ast.parse(SIBLING.read_text()), "CrewAIAgentComponent", "build_output")
sibling_llm = kwarg_src(find_agent_call(fn), "llm")
print(f"sibling llm kwarg          = {sibling_llm!r}")
assert "convert_llm" in sibling_llm

# Check 3: downstream wrapper lazy-converts (only fires for SequentialCrew consumers)
fn = find_method(ast.parse(WRAPPER.read_text()), "BaseCrewComponent", "get_tasks_and_agents")
lazy_wrap_found = any(
    isinstance(s, ast.Assign) and isinstance(s.targets[0], ast.Attribute)
    and s.targets[0].attr == "llm" and isinstance(s.targets[0].value, ast.Name)
    and s.targets[0].value.id == "agent" and "convert_llm" in ast.unparse(s.value)
    for s in ast.walk(fn)
)
print(f"downstream lazy wrap found = {lazy_wrap_found}")
assert lazy_wrap_found

print("CONFIRMED BUG: SequentialTaskAgentComponent skips convert_llm; sibling does not")
```



### Expected behavior

Either (a) both `SequentialTaskAgentComponent.build_agent_and_task` and `CrewAIAgentComponent.build_output` apply `convert_llm` eagerly at the `Agent(...)` construction site, or (b) the `SequentialTaskAgentComponent` is removed since it is already marked `legacy = True`. The current state — one factory wraps, the other does not — leaves Agents produced by the legacy component with a non-CrewAI `.llm` whenever the Agent is consumed outside the `SequentialCrew` lazy-wrap path.


### Who can help?

_No response_

### Operating System

Ubuntu Linux 22.04

### Langflow Version

1.10.0 (bug is in source at commit `43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8`, current `main`)

### Python Version

3.12

title: [BUG] CrewAI wrappers mutate shared Agent.llm/.tools across crews and across tasks
author: Fr3ya
body:
### Bug Description

`BaseCrewComponent.get_tasks_and_agents` at [`src/lfx/src/lfx/base/agents/crewai/crew.py:158-169`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/base/agents/crewai/crew.py#L158-L169) walks the agents list and rewrites each agent's `llm` and `tools` in place, with no defensive copy upstream:

```python
def get_tasks_and_agents(self, agents_list=None) -> tuple[list, list]:
    if not agents_list:
        agents_list = self.agents or []
    for agent in agents_list:
        agent.llm = convert_llm(agent.llm)        # in-place rebind on the shared Agent
        agent.tools = convert_tools(agent.tools)  # in-place rebind on the shared Agent
    return self.tasks, agents_list
```

Two related call sites compound the problem in the visual graph:

1. [`CrewAIAgentComponent.build_output`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/crewai.py#L83-L108) returns one `crewai.Agent` object whose `llm` / `tools` are the wired handles; that one object is what flows through every visual edge from the Agent node.
2. [`SequentialCrewComponent.agents`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_crew.py#L19-L30) derives agents from each task with no `id`-based dedup, so an Agent referenced by N `SequentialTask` nodes appears N times in the list mutated by the loop above.



### Reproduction

```python

from __future__ import annotations
import ast, sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

# --- stand-ins for crewai.Agent / LLM / Tool (only fields the bug touches) ---
@dataclass
class FakeTool: name: str; wrapper_layers: int = 0
@dataclass
class FakeLLM:  model: str; wrapper_layers: int = 0
@dataclass
class FakeAgent:
    role: str; llm: FakeLLM
    tools: list = field(default_factory=list)
@dataclass
class FakeTask: name: str; agent: FakeAgent

def convert_llm(llm):  # mirrors lfx/base/agents/crewai/crew.py:convert_llm
    return None if llm is None else FakeLLM(llm.model, llm.wrapper_layers + 1)
def convert_tools(tools):
    return [FakeTool(t.name, t.wrapper_layers + 1) for t in (tools or [])]

class MutatingCrew:  # mirrors BaseCrewComponent.get_tasks_and_agents (crew.py:158-169)
    def __init__(self, tasks, agents_list=None):
        self.tasks, self._agents_list = tasks, agents_list
    def get_tasks_and_agents(self, agents_list=None):
        if not agents_list:
            agents_list = self._agents_list or []
        for agent in agents_list:
            agent.llm = convert_llm(agent.llm)        # the buggy in-place writes
            agent.tools = convert_tools(agent.tools)
        return self.tasks, agents_list

class MutatingSequentialCrew(MutatingCrew):  # mirrors SequentialCrewComponent (sequential_crew.py:19-30)
    @property
    def agents(self):
        return [t.agent for t in self.tasks if hasattr(t, "agent")]  # no dedup
    def get_tasks_and_agents(self, agents_list=None):
        if not agents_list:
            agents_list = self.agents + (agents_list or [])
        return super().get_tasks_and_agents(agents_list=agents_list)

# Demo 1: one Agent node wired into two Crews -> mutation propagates across crews
shared = FakeAgent("researcher", FakeLLM("gpt-4o"), [FakeTool("search")])
MutatingCrew([FakeTask("a", shared)], [shared]).get_tasks_and_agents()
MutatingCrew([FakeTask("b", shared)], [shared]).get_tasks_and_agents()
assert shared.llm.wrapper_layers == 2, shared.llm.wrapper_layers

# Demo 2: one Agent shared by 3 SequentialTasks -> 3x mutation in one crew build
shared = FakeAgent("reviewer", FakeLLM("gpt-4o"), [FakeTool("read")])
crew = MutatingSequentialCrew([
    FakeTask("r1", shared), FakeTask("r2", shared), FakeTask("r3", shared),
])
crew.get_tasks_and_agents()
assert shared.llm.wrapper_layers == 3, shared.llm.wrapper_layers

print("CONFIRMED BUG:shared-agent in-place mutation across crews/tasks")
```

Running this prints `CONFIRMED BUG:  shared-agent in-place mutation across crews/tasks` and exits 0.



### Expected behavior

`get_tasks_and_agents` should leave each input Agent's `llm` and `tools` attributes untouched. Each Crew that consumes a shared Agent should operate on a per-crew copy (`agent.model_copy(deep=True)`), and `SequentialCrewComponent.agents` should dedup by `id(agent)` so an Agent referenced by N tasks is converted at most once per build.


### Who can help?

_No response_

### Operating System

Ubuntu Linux 22.04

### Langflow Version

1.10.0 (bug is in source at commit `43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8`, current `main`)

### Python Version

3.12

title: [BUG] CrewAI build_crew wires self.agents/tasks/memory into Crew; component reused across requests
author: Fr3ya
body:
### Bug Description

`SequentialCrewComponent.build_crew` ([`src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_crew.py:32-53`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_crew.py#L32-L53)) and `HierarchicalCrewComponent.build_crew` ([`src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/hierarchical_crew.py:23-47`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/hierarchical_crew.py#L23-L47)) both construct a `crewai.Crew` whose `agents` / `tasks` / `memory` keyword arguments are instance attributes on `self`: `agents` and `tasks` come back from `self.get_tasks_and_agents()` ([crew.py:158-169](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/base/agents/crewai/crew.py#L158-L169) reads from `self.agents` / `self.tasks`), and `memory=self.memory` is passed through verbatim. No deep copy is performed before the Crew is constructed.

`Vertex._build` at [`src/lfx/src/lfx/graph/vertex/base.py:382-418`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/lfx/src/lfx/graph/vertex/base.py#L382-L418) takes an `else: custom_component = self.custom_component` branch once the component is instantiated, and the enclosing `Graph` is cached by `flow_id` in `chat_service` ([`src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/chat.py:163`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8/src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/chat.py#L163)). By Python reference semantics, two `build_crew()` invocations on the same cached component return two `Crew` objects whose `agents[i]`, `tasks[i]`, and `memory` are the same Python objects. When `memory=True`, the memory store is reused across consumers.



### Reproduction

The reproducer below: (1) AST-parses the real source at commit `43d3510e` and asserts that `Crew(agents=..., tasks=..., memory=self.memory)` is wired from `self`-backed values in both subclasses, plus that `Vertex._build` has the reuse branch; (2) replays the lifecycle with stand-in `FakeCrew` / `FakeAgent` / `FakeTask` / `FakeMemory` classes and asserts object identity across two invocations of the cached component's `build_crew`.

```python

from __future__ import annotations
import ast, sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

REPO = Path("/path/to/cloned/langflow")     # adjust to your checkout
SEQ  = REPO/"src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/sequential_crew.py"
HIER = REPO/"src/lfx/src/lfx/components/crewai/hierarchical_crew.py"
VTX  = REPO/"src/lfx/src/lfx/graph/vertex/base.py"

def kwarg_src(call, name):
    for kw in call.keywords:
        if kw.arg == name: return ast.unparse(kw.value)
    return None

def crew_kwargs(path):
    for n in ast.walk(ast.parse(path.read_text())):
        if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and isinstance(n.func, ast.Name) and n.func.id == "Crew":
            return {k: kwarg_src(n, k) for k in ("agents","tasks","memory")}
    return {}

def vertex_reuses_instance(path):
    for n in ast.walk(ast.parse(path.read_text())):
        if isinstance(n, ast.AsyncFunctionDef) and n.name == "_build":
            for c in ast.walk(n):
                if (isinstance(c, ast.Assign) and isinstance(c.targets[0], ast.Name)
                    and c.targets[0].id == "custom_component"
                    and isinstance(c.value, ast.Attribute)
                    and isinstance(c.value.value, ast.Name) and c.value.value.id == "self"
                    and c.value.attr == "custom_component"):
                    return True
    return False

print("SequentialCrew  Crew(...) kwargs:", crew_kwargs(SEQ))
print("HierarchicalCrew Crew(...) kwargs:", crew_kwargs(HIER))
print("Vertex._build reuses self.custom_component:", vertex_reuses_instance(VTX))

# --- stand-ins to demonstrate the resulting object-identity sharing ---
@dataclass
class FakeMemory: history: list = field(default_factory=list)
@dataclass
class FakeAgent:  role: str
@dataclass
class FakeTask:   description: str; agent: FakeAgent
@dataclass
class FakeCrew:   agents: list; tasks: list; memory: object

class CachedComponentInstance:           # mirrors BaseCrewComponent's relevant shape
    def __init__(self, agents, tasks, *, memory):
        self.agents, self.tasks = agents, tasks
        self.memory = FakeMemory() if memory else False
    def build_crew(self):                # mirrors SequentialCrewComponent.build_crew
        return FakeCrew(agents=self.agents, tasks=self.tasks, memory=self.memory)

a = FakeAgent("researcher")
t = FakeTask("answer", a)
component = CachedComponentInstance([a], [t], memory=True)

crew_alice = component.build_crew()
crew_alice.memory.history.append("alice: secret 123-45-6789")
crew_bob   = component.build_crew()
assert crew_alice.agents[0] is crew_bob.agents[0]
assert crew_alice.tasks[0]  is crew_bob.tasks[0]
assert crew_alice.memory    is crew_bob.memory
assert "alice" in crew_bob.memory.history[0]
```

### Expected behavior

Each invocation of `build_crew()` should return a `Crew` whose `agents`, `tasks`, and `memory` are independent of any prior or concurrent invocation on the same component instance. Concretely: deep-copy the agents and tasks list per call, and either materialise a fresh memory store per call or document that `memory=True` is unsafe under the cached-component lifecycle. Alternatively, opt the CrewAI components out of the vertex's `self.custom_component` reuse so a fresh component is built per request.


### Who can help?

_No response_

### Operating System

Ubuntu Linux 22.04

### Langflow Version

1.10.0 (bug is in source at commit `43d3510ec74ad3125f1e1e0b5598900c0e0e70f8`, current `main`)

### Python Version

None

title: [Bug]: Silent Swallowing of asyncio.CancelledError in memory.py Causes Zombie Coroutines and System Resource Starvation
author: QiuYucheng2003
body:
### Bug Description

In src/backend/base/langflow/memory.py, the aadd_messagetables function catches asyncio.CancelledError but completely fails to re-raise it. This is a severe asyncio anti-pattern.

When the scheduler or agent sends a cancellation signal (e.g., due to timeout, quota limits, or safe-guards), the code rolls back the database session but then either suppresses the signal via a recursive retry or translates it into a standard ValueError. In the Python asyncio ecosystem, silently swallowing a CancelledError breaks the task group cancellation chain. The event loop is misled into believing the task completed normally. Under high-concurrency, long-running agent interactions, this causes the underlying coroutines to escape the task manager's control, becoming "zombie coroutines" that spin aimlessly and eventually exhaust the host's CPU time slices and memory.

### Reproduction

Because this is an underlying architectural state bug, it occurs under load when task cancellations are triggered (e.g., via build_public_tmp).

Code Snippet showing the exact vulnerability:

# Location: src/backend/base/langflow/memory.py -> aadd_messagetables

        # ...
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            await session.rollback()
            if retry_count >= max_retries:
                await logger.awarning(...)
                error_msg = "Add Message operation cancelled after multiple retries"
                # BUG: Swallows CancelledError by raising a standard ValueError
                raise ValueError(error_msg) from None 
            # BUG: Returns retry result, silencing the cancellation signal to the event loop
            return await aadd_messagetables(messages, session, retry_count + 1)
            
    except asyncio.CancelledError as e:
        await logger.aexception(e)
        error_msg = "Operation cancelled"
        # BUG: Translates CancelledError to ValueError, breaking the async cancellation chain
        raise ValueError(error_msg) from e

### Expected behavior

When asyncio.CancelledError is caught, the function should perform its necessary cleanup (such as await session.rollback()) and then strictly re-raise the CancelledError (or let it propagate). It should not be wrapped into a ValueError or suppressed via returning a retry loop, ensuring the asyncio event loop can properly terminate the task hierarchy and garbage collect the resources.

### Who can help?

@italojohnny

### Operating System

macos

### Langflow Version

main

### Python Version

3.11

### Screenshot

(Not applicable - Core asynchronous logic bug)

(Not applicable)
